title: offsetof macro should be avoided
description: "offsetof" macro should not be usedAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode Smellmisra-c++2008misra-c2004suspiciousWhy is this an issue?More Infooffsetofcan lead to undefined behavior when the argument types are incompatible or when bit fields are used. Thereforeoffsetofshould be avoided.Noncompliant code example#include <stddef.h> struct A { int32_t i; }; void f1 ( ) { offsetof ( A, i ); // Noncompliant }Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Policy|Cookie Policy|Terms of Use
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

noncompliant: size_t bitfield_offset = offsetof(MyStruct, bitfield); // Noncompliant: undefined with bitfields
offsetof(IncompleteType, member); // Noncompliant: undefined with incomplete types
compliant: // Use container_of macro for pointer arithmetic instead
// Define custom offset calculation for specific use cases

checks.message: offsetof macro should be avoided. Use manual pointer arithmetic or alternative approaches for struct member offset calculation to avoid undefined behavior.
